title: Guinness Carrot Bredie – Now What Could Be Nicer?
excerpt: By now everyone knows that on a Monday I just want to make food quickly. I bought a bunch of carrots at Fruit and Veg and decided to make the carrot bredie with dark beer (Guinness). The bredie came out soft and asked for more and more and more …

seo.title: Guinness Carrot Bredie - Now What Could Be Nicer? - Marinda Kook

html:
By now everyone knows that on a Monday I just want to make food quickly. I bought a bunch of carrots at Fruit and Veg and decided to make the carrot bredie with dark beer (Guinness). The bredie came out soft and asked for more and more and more ……….

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 2 kg stewing beef – I used brisket and short rib
  • 1 Guinness beer
  • 1 tin tomatoes
  • 500 ml stock, made up from a stock cube
  • 5 cloves
  • 8 large carrots
  • 4 potatoes
  • salt and pepper
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • 1 onion
  • 60 ml oil
  • 80 ml Worcester sauce

NOW:

Slice the onions into rings and fry lightly in the oil

Cut the meat into cubes and brown together with the onions.

Cut the carrots into rounds and the potatoes into cubes and add to the meat

Add the tin of tomatoes to the meat

Mix the rest of the ingredients and add them in.

Now let the bredie simmer slowly until it's soft and cooked through. Taste all the way through and add whatever your tongue asks for.

It's delicious. You can thicken the sauce with Bisto or Maizena if you feel like it. I served it with rice. The next day the bredie actually tastes even nicer!
